references.title: Using learning analytics to explore help-seeking learner profiles in MOOCs

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Sample only includes students who achieved at least 40% of the course grade.

Feature matrix was normalized and, subsequently followed by a t-SNE before clustering.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Low Engagement: Low grades, low activities, No forum threads/posts

Assessment-Focused - low grades: high attempts of assessments, low video interactions, low pageviews

Passive Engagement: Meduim grades, moderate pageviews, low activity on forum

Acitve Engagement: High Engagement, moderate aassessment attempts, high forum participation

Assessment-focused - high grades: High grades, high assessments, low pageviews and video
extracted.Implications: MOOC designers need to make interactions with forums and/or learning contents more engaging for learners… otherwise these will only be passively used.
extracted.Comments: Authors use Affinity Propagation to identify different patterns of help-seeking-behavior from MOOC data.

annotations.text: The ability to reach tutors or peers is often hindered in MOOCs in comparison to traditional settings. The authors look to identify different types of help-seeking behavior in MOOCs from Melbourne University. To do that, they use different unsupervised techniques (affinity propagation clustering and t-SNE). Authors identify 5 patterns of help-seeking behavior and evaluate the relationship of these profiles with performance.
